sections.paragraph:
m. constr. זִכְרֹון pl. ־ִים and ֹות.

(1) memory, remembrance, Jos 4:7 Exo 12:14 Ecc 1:11 2:16 . אַבְנֵי זִכָּרֹון memorial stones, the name applied to the two gems in the shoulder bands with which the dress of the high priest was adorned, Exo 28:12 39:7 . מִנְחַת זִכָּרֹון a memorial offering, Num 5:15 שׂוּם זִכָּרֹון to establish a memorial, namely by the procreation of children, Isa 57:8. [?]

(2) a memorial or memento, ὑπόμνημα (French mémoire). Exo 17:14, כְּתֹב זאֹת זִכָּרֹון בַּסֵּפֶר “write this a memorial (that which shall cause to be remembered) in a book.” סֵפֶר זִכָּרֹון Mal 3:16, and pl. סֵפֶר הַזִּכְרֹונֹות Est 6:1, a book of memorials, annals, journals; comp. דָּכְרָן; a memorial sign, Exo 13:9.

(3) the celebration of any particular day (comp. the verb, Est 9:28 Exo 20:8); Lev 23:24.

(4) i.q. מָשָׁל a memorial sentence, ἀπόφθεγμα, Job 13:12.
